rbaHistory.note: First cut since Nov 2020 — CPI back in 2-3% band
Held; trimmed mean CPI above target
Held; Q1 CPI surprised to upside

news.title: ASX edges down; bank stocks slide but tech firms surge
news.snippet: The Australian sharemarket bucked the trend of rising global markets to close lower on Thursday.

news.title: Huge push to spare housing from feared tax
news.snippet: One type of homes could be spared from tax reforms currently being considered by the Albanese government.

news.title: Dalaroo scores stakeholder backing for West African gold hunt
news.snippet: Dalaroo Metals has boosted its hunt for gold in Côte d’Ivoire, securing local support to fast-track soil sampling across a 9.5km trend at its Bondoukou project.

news.title: There’s a war raging! Why is the US sharemarket at all-time highs?
news.snippet: It’s not that the US market didn’t get the war memo, it’s just that it has decided to ignore Trump’s bluster.

news.title: Aurum unlocks deeper gold 220m below existing West African resource
news.snippet: Aurum Resources has drilled to 220m vertically below its current BST1 resource at Boundiali in Côte d’Ivoire, confirming the gold system remains open at depth.

news.title: NSW nurses and midwives land major payrise
news.snippet: A landmark payrise for nurses and midwives in NSW will cost “billions”, the state’s treasurer has warned, but says the investment is crucial.

news.title: Government’s Iran response must be ‘fiscally responsible’: statement
news.snippet: Australia’s Treasurer has signed an extraordinary joint statement with US allies warning governments to be “responsible” as fallout from the Iran war grows.

news.title: Big problem with Albo’s huge tax move
news.snippet: Next month’s budget will probably include cuts to the capital gains tax or negative gearing – or both. And it could backfire spectacularly.

news.title: Union’s move on fuel vouchers as apprentices feel the pinch
news.snippet: Thousands of workers will be eligible for $100 fuel vouchers, as cost of living pressures put Australia’s housing future at risk.

news.title: There’s a silent epidemic in our workplaces, and WFH is to blame
news.snippet: Spending time with our colleagues is something we once took for granted, but in the modern workplace, that’s no longer the case.

news.title: ‘No explanation’: Probe into suspicious trades
news.snippet: A “pattern” of suspiciously timed, highly lucrative trades, all made just before major announcements on the Iran War, is being investigated.

news.title: First signs of Mideast conflict hit Australian jobs
news.snippet: Thousands of Australians entered the workforce at the start of the year, but experts warn it could be the calm before the storm and rates may rise again.
